meta.title: Production of vesicular stomatitis virus with low infectivity by interferon-treated cells -- Maheshwari and Friedman 44 (1): 261 -- Journal of General Virology

abstractHtml: In cultures of Ly cells treated with 10 or 30 reference units/ml of mouse interferon there was a 30 to 200 times reduction in the production of infectious vesicular stomatitis virus (VSV); virus particle production, as measured by VSV particle associated virus RNA, virus protein, and virus transcriptase, was inhibited by, at most, six times. These results suggested that interferon-treated cells produce VSV particles with low infectivity and resemble the findings in interferon-treated cells infected with murine leukaemia viruses.
